items.reviewerNotes: Codex low-risk approveExistingQid: Image is a triangular warning sign showing a tunnel; French option D = 'tunnel' matches master q0734 meaning. Local option order differs from master, so localized letter is D.
Codex low-risk approveExistingQid: Green/white rectangular 'P' sign with arrow indicates highway parking area ahead; French option D corresponds to 'parking autoroutier' and matches q0895.

Codex high-risk createNewQuestion: Create new: source shows a blue U-turn permitted sign and asks whether you cannot make a U-turn here; the answer is No/B. The closest q0202 uses a different prohibitory-sign image/meaning, so approving it would collapse opposite sign semantics.

Codex medium-risk createNewQuestion: Brown rectangular tourist sign with numeric distance (16 km) clearly indicates 'distance to tourist area' — French option B. No single high-confidence existing qid clearly matches (top candidates conflict), so create new.

Codex medium-risk createNewQuestion: Round blue sign with two white arrows left and right indicates that turning left and right is allowed — French option C. Candidate pool shows mixed matches; not a clearly identical existing MCQ, so create new.

Codex low-risk approveExistingQid: Statement that driver must use high beams when meeting another vehicle on narrow roads/bridges at night is incorrect; correct answer is No (B). q0269 matches the same proposition and is marked 'Wrong' — approve mapping.
Codex low-risk approveExistingQid: Statement that max speed when crossing narrow passage/bridge is 30 km/h is consistent with q0288. For ROW, French 'Yes' is option A and corresponds to 'Right'.
Codex low-risk approveExistingQid: Red 'P' dashboard indicator denotes parking/handbrake engaged. French option B 'levier de frein a main est en marche' matches q0601 meaning (handbrake engaged).

Codex medium-risk createNewQuestion: Yellow airbag warning lamp illuminated continuously indicates a fault (not that system is working) — correct answer No (B). Top machine matches reference unrelated indicators; no clear existing qid matches, so create new. Rejected top matcher: Top machine candidates correspond to other dashboard indicators (trunk/hood/open-door), conflicting with airbag symbol in source image.

Codex low-risk approveExistingQid: Source ROW statement (rear tire blowout: hold wheel with both hands, keep straight, reduce speed) semantically matches q0062 (machine top match = Right). Source 'Yes' = option A maps to Right.

Codex low-risk approveExistingQid: Embedded circular prohibitory sign shows straight+right-turn arrows with red slash; q0867 top candidate meaning 'no going straight and no right turn' matches. Source correct key D corresponds to that meaning.

Codex medium-risk keepUnresolved: Source claims using another vehicle's registration and plate 'accumulates 3 points'. Top machine candidate (q0322) flags this statement as Wrong, but legal/penalty specifics are uncertain from available evidence—insufficient to approve.

Codex medium-risk approveExistingQid: Image (night sharp curve) and prompt about alternating high/low beams align with q0287 (machine top match = Right). Some candidates suggest low beam only, so confidence is moderate.
Codex low-risk approveExistingQid: Machine top q0402 indicates 'make a U-turn at broken central lines'. Source option D ('faire demi-tour contre la ligne discontinue du milieu') matches that meaning.

Codex medium-risk keepUnresolved: Dashboard fan icon likely indicates the ventilation/blower fan (machine q0597 = 'cooling or warming fan'), but none of the source options precisely match that meaning (A = cold-air fan is narrower). Options mismatch => unresolved.

Codex medium-risk keepUnresolved: Blue rectangular sign with two large arrows left/right is visually clear but the provided options (types of intersection: roundabout/cross/T/Y) do not map unambiguously to the sign; candidate matches are low/conflicting.
Codex low-risk approveExistingQid: Source statement about front tire blowout being more dangerous matches q0100 (machine top = Right). Source 'Yes' = option A maps to Right.
Codex low-risk approveExistingQid: Dashboard top-view car icon with both side doors open corresponds to q0602 meaning 'doors of both sides are open'; source option A matches that meaning.

Codex medium-risk approveExistingQid: Triangular warning sign with car driving through water indicates a flooded/submersible section. q0681 ('flooded road') matches this semantics; source option A ('tronson submersible') corresponds to that meaning (return source letter A).

Codex low-risk approveExistingQid: Image shows white rectangular parking bays parallel to the curb; master q0691 ('horizontal/parallel parking space') matches meaning and correct option maps to French B.

Codex medium-risk approveExistingQid: Blue rectangular sign with main-arrow and side lines indicates 'priority to main road'; master q0769 corresponds to this meaning. French option C ('priorité à la voie principale') matches.

Codex low-risk approveExistingQid: Text asks what to do when meeting a bicycle at night; q0139 ('use low beam, reduce speed or stop') matches French option D exactly. Chose q0139 over top-ranked q0511 because q0139 matches encounter-with-bike scenario. Rejected top matcher: Top machine match (q0511) addresses overtaking lighting, not the specific 'encountering a bicycle' scenario; q0139 is a closer semantic match.

Codex medium-risk approveExistingQid: Statement about max speed 50km/h on urban road without center line aligns with master q0191 which labels that statement as Wrong; therefore French 'Non' (B) is the localized correct answer. Moderate confidence due to regional/regulatory nuance.
Codex low-risk approveExistingQid: Question asks where overtaking is prohibited; master q0501 correctOption ('narrow bridge and curve') maps to French D ('routes et ponts étroits').
Codex low-risk approveExistingQid: Interior photo shows center console gear stick; master q0578 identifies this as the gear lever. French option C ('le levier de vitesse') matches.
Codex low-risk approveExistingQid: Scenario with pedestrians in rain; master q0167 ('honk in advance and reduce speed') corresponds to French D. Good semantic match.
Codex low-risk approveExistingQid: Statement about accelerating as fast as possible and immediately turning left is unsafe; master q0098 marks this as Wrong. French 'Non' (B) corresponds.

Codex medium-risk approveExistingQid: Sign shows train icon with '50' numeric tag; master q0682 correct meaning is 'unmanned crossing 50m ahead'. Master option ordering differs; French option A ('50 m avant le passage à niveau non surveillé') matches that meaning.

Codex medium-risk approveExistingQid: Image shows ignition key positions; statement 'ACC position starts engine' is false. Master q0558 explicitly covers starter not working at ACC and marks it Wrong — maps to French 'Non' (B). Chosen q0558 though top-ranked q0539 addressed START position rather than ACC. Rejected top matcher: Top machine match q0539 concerns START position causing starter to run; the ACC-specific statement is better matched by q0558 (rank 2).

Codex medium-risk keepUnresolved: Dashboard icon is a green headlamp-like symbol; source key C (position lights) conflicts with machine candidates (front fog / sidelights / low beam). Visual semantics ambiguous between dipped/head/position lights — insufficient confidence to map to existing qid.

Codex low-risk approveExistingQid: Statement says you may honk and accelerate to bypass bicycles — traffic-safety rule forbids this. Matches q0028 (Wrong). Source binary option B = No corresponds to Wrong.
Codex low-risk approveExistingQid: Asking behavior on uneven road; correct is to reduce speed and pass slowly. Matches q0117 (A).
Codex low-risk approveExistingQid: Statement: reduce speed at night if oncoming vehicle keeps high beam on to avoid pedestrian accidents. Matches q0017 (Right). Source Yes = A.
Codex low-risk approveExistingQid: Statement claims no need to use turn signal when merging right — this is false. Matches q0227 (Wrong). Source No = B.

Codex high-risk keepUnresolved: Night intersection image; options include 'turn off high beams' and 'alternate high/low'. Candidate q0465 suggests alternating, but visual/context and action ambiguity (when to dip vs flash) create conflict — insufficiently clear to approve a qid or answer.
Codex low-risk approveExistingQid: Question asks type of yellow broken center line; correct = indicative/indicative marking. Matches existing qid (indicative marking). Source option A.
Codex low-risk approveExistingQid: Statement claims pushing the stalk down turns on right indicator — false (direction inverted). Matches q0529 (Wrong). Source No = B.
Codex low-risk approveExistingQid: Statement says follow traffic lights when they contradict police — legally police override signals, so statement is false. Matches q0225 (Wrong). Source No = B.

Codex medium-risk createNewQuestion: Create new: source asks about pedestrians on both sides with water on the road; option C is to reduce speed and yield. q0105 has similar caution answer logic but a different scenario (another vehicle cutting in/taking the lane), so MCQ option-row/context matching is not safe enough for an existing-qid approval.
Codex low-risk createNewQuestion: No close existing candidate; correct answer is clearly 'réduire la visibilité' (D) for fog. Recommend creating new question with D.
Codex low-risk approveExistingQid: q0084 matches the assertion that smoking while driving has no impact — that assertion is false. Approve existing qid with answer No (B).
